text: Fill in the blanks to test your knowledge of minerals, electrolytes, and acid-base balance.
questions: The master regulator of iron homeostasis is *hepcidin*, a peptide hormone made by the *liver* that binds and internalises *ferroportin*.
Iron is absorbed in the *duodenum* and proximal jejunum; non-haem iron absorption is enhanced by *Vitamin C/ascorbic acid* and inhibited by *phytates/tannins*.
The best single marker to distinguish iron deficiency anaemia from anaemia of chronic disease is serum *ferritin*, which is low in IDA and normal or high in ACD.
The Henderson-Hasselbalch equation states that pH equals *pKa* plus the log of the ratio of conjugate *base/A minus* to weak *acid/HA*.
*Transferrin* is the plasma transport protein for iron, carrying *two/2* Fe³⁺ ions per molecule, and TIBC is *high/elevated* in iron deficiency anaemia.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review iron metabolism, hepcidin regulation, and acid-base fundamentals.
Good effort! Revisit the diagnostic markers for IDA vs ACD and buffer systems.
Excellent! You have a strong understanding of minerals, electrolytes, and acid-base balance.
